articles.title: The Forge We Deserve
articles.summary: GitHub is facing a crisis as its uptime and stability issues have become a major concern, leading high-profile projects like Ghostty to move away from the platform. Developers are starting to discuss alternative options, with some opting for Git forges that offer more control over their data and ecosystem. A promising path ahead is being built on an open, interoperable protocol called AT Protocol, which allows users to self-host their data and aggregate it in one centralized location. This could provide a structurally resistant solution to the lock-in issues plaguing GitHub, enabling developers to run their own forks with greater freedom.

articles.title: CLI Authentication, the Right Way
articles.summary: CLI authentication uses an HTTP server on 127.0.0.1 at some port, such as 8085 or 8976, to handle OAuth authorization requests from the client-side browser. This process involves exchanging an authorization code with the token endpoint and shutting down the local server when a successful login is achieved. The localhost step is often invisible in the query string of the authorization URL, but its presence is crucial for seamless authentication.

articles.title: About ASCII art and Jgs font (2023)
articles.summary: ASCII art is a form of digital art that uses characters from a standard code to create images, blurring the distinction between text and image in both the art world and informatics domain. ASCII art originated with Joan G. Stark's self-portrait featuring her signature, which has become one of the most popular and prolific examples of the style. The term "ASCII Art" or just "ASCII" refers to any picture created using typographic elements, often limited to symbols and characters available on a computer keyboard.
